title: Task-Type Definition
description: Framework §5 / Discovery Records. One schema read three ways: dispatch input, capture form, and acceptance criteria. Each slot has all three readings. Promotion of a discovery record for a known type is review-and-accept because the record is already in this shape.

blueprint.description: Home blueprint (first home; may be reusable).

classification.description: Tactical-DDD placement, drives event-model depth and dispatchability.

does_not_apply_when.description: Boundaries; when to route elsewhere (core task type or broad worker).

slots.description: The dual-read slots. Each is simultaneously a dispatch input, a capture question, and an acceptance check.

kind.description: Which framework layer the slot's delta belongs to.

dispatch.description: What the generator needs this slot for.

audit.description: What the verification checks for this slot.

cells.description: The work units (SPMC) this task type dispatches.

derived_from.description: Frozen input pointers (domain concepts, contract conventions, other cells).

edits.description: Relative path of an existing file this cell edits (e.g. wiring a `pub mod` declaration) instead of producing a new artifact.

audits.description: The verifications that make the definition true. Each names the slot/principle it protects (no slot without a backing audit).

protects.description: The slot or principle this audit backs.

deltas.description: The shape of the discovery-record diff a session of this type produces.
